source_paper.authors: Jermilova, U., Macdonell, C., Chapman, P.M., Vander Meulen, I.J., et al.
source_paper.title: Bayesian Network Relative Risk Model for Mercury in the Mackenzie River Basin

reconstruction_method.summary: Independent reconstruction from published data, R code, and supplementary materials. DAG structure from Figure 2, CPTs learned via BDeu (ESS=1.0) from FRDR training data, deterministic nodes from Dillon 2010 dose-response and Canadian dietary guidelines.
reconstruction_method.cpt_learning: BDeu (ESS=1.0) for stressor nodes; deterministic mappings for effect/endpoint nodes
reconstruction_method.discretization_source: Table S4 (supplementary) + R code breakpoint definitions

key_differences_from_canonical: Mercury-only pathway (vs multi-metal + organic in Landis)
Fish tissue and human health endpoints (vs benthic community in Landis)
Freshwater system (vs marine/estuarine sediment in Landis)
Region-specific point sources (mine proximity vs direct contamination levels)
Dietary exposure and pTWI exceedance endpoints (not present in Landis)
